LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS ASPIRE ROCK LLC AND EASY.LY INC.'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND FOR SANCTIONS - THE MOTION TO VACATE THE DEFAULTS OF ASPIRE ROCK, LLC AND EASY.LY INC. IS GRANTED. BOTH DEFENDANTS FILED THIS MOTION IN A TIMELY FASHION, PROVIDE A PROPOSED PLEADING, AND DEMONSTRATE EXCUSABLE NEGLECT AND/OR MISTAKE. EASY.LY DID NOT RESPOND TO THE COMPLAINT IN A TIMELY FASHION BECAUSE MR. ROTH RECEIVED THE SERVICE PACKAGE AFTER DEFAULT WAS ENTERED. MR. ROTH'S BELIEF THAT ASPIRE ROCK, LLC WAS NOT PROPERLY SERVED WAS A REASONABLE MISTAKE OF LAW. THE COURT DENIES THE REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-29-2014 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Or Dismiss
Off Calendar Nov-04-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KYLE L. SCHRINER OF THE SCHRINER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 321-4924 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ASPIRE ROCK LLC and EASY.LY INC.'s Motion To Stay Or Dismiss is granted. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. A CCP 430.10 motion is the proper procedure for enforcing a forum selection clause. (See Cal-State Business Products & Services, Inc. v. Ricoh (1993) 12 Cal.App.4th 1666, 1680, "a defendant may enforce a forum-selection clause by bringing a motion pursuant to sections 410.30 and 418.10, the statutes governing forum non conveniens motions'") The forum selection clause is mandatory and therefore, will be given effect unless enforcement would be unreasonable. (Intershop Communications v. Superior Court (2002) 104 Cal.App.4th 191, 196.) Plaintiffs have not established that enforcement would be unreasonable. In the alternative, issue preclusion applies to the determination of whether the forum selection clause is reasonable, as all of the factors for issue preclusion enumerated by the California Supreme Court are met here. (Lucido v. Sup. Ct. (1990) 51 Cal.3d 335, 341.) The New York court has already ruled on this issue. Additionally, Defendants did not waive their right to bring this motion, nor is this motion untimely. A reasonableness standard governs the time that a CCP 410.30 motion must be brought, and Defendants brought this motion within a reasonable time. (See Trident Labs v. Merrill Lynch Comm. Fin. Corp. ((2011) 200 Cal.App.4th 147, 155.) Enforcing the forum selection clause does not violate public policy, as both California and New York recognize the enforceability of forum selection clauses. (See Swenson v. T-Mobile USA, Inc. (S.D. Cal. 2006) 415 F.Supp.2d 1101, 1105, "the question is not whether the application of the forum's law would violate the policy of the other party's state, but rather, whether enforcement of the forum selection agreement would violate the policy of the other party's state as to the forum for litigation of the dispute.") ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-22-2013 Set For Trial On Jun-02-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion For Default Court Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-27-2013 Off Calendar

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Jun-21-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RODRIGO FUNG, TEL. NO. (408) 507-8295 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. KYLE L. SCHRINER OF THE SCHRINER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 321-4892 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ASPIRE ROCK LLC and EASY.LY INC.'s MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND FOR SANCTIONS - The motion to vacate the defaults of Aspire Rock, LLC and Easy.Ly Inc. is granted. Both defendants filed this motion in a timely fashion, provide a proposed pleading, and demonstrate excusable neglect and/or mistake. Easy.Ly did not respond to the complaint in a timely fashion because Mr. Roth received the service package after default was entered. Mr. Roth's belief that Aspire Rock, LLC was not properly served was a reasonable mistake of law. The Court denies the requests for sanctions. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
